title: [Ovarian cancer: molecular pathology and molecularly targeted therapy].
author_string: Reis, Henning, Völkl, Lisa, Demes, Melanie, Moinfar, Farid, Wild, Peter J
abstract: Ovarian cancer is a common malignant genital neoplasm in women. Due to its frequent diagnosis only in advanced stages, prognosis is poor, although biomarker-driven targeted therapeutic approaches are evolving. These are currently based on molecular pathological analyses of homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) and the BRCA1/2 mutational status. The current CME article focuses on the histo- and molecular pathology of ovarian cancer, relevant molecular mechanisms, and test systems. These are discussed in the context of biomarker-driven targeted therapy.
